response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but this role's mandatory pillars are almost entirely unmet. His professional C/C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014–2015, far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++. His database background is MySQL and MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and he has no fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking exposure — RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech product, not a capital-markets system. The gap between his profile (TypeScript/Node, DLang, full-stack) and this specialization is too wide to bridge for a consulting engagement expecting immediate domain-ready C++ delivery.
strengths: Deep, broad engineering experience including low-latency, high-throughput systems work (DLang at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Excellent communication skills evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and course creation
Solid software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, DevOps tooling, and open-source contributions
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C/C++ experience, a decade ago, versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL/MongoDB
No fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain exposure
Strong general engineering and communication skills
